criteria_verification.criterion_text: `src/jarvis/agents/subagents/jarvis_reasoner.py` exposes a module-level `graph: CompiledStateGraph` via `create_deep_agent(model="openai:jarvis-reasoner", system_prompt=<resolved>, tools=[])`. Compilation happens at import time per DDR-012 — `from jarvis.agents.subagents.jarvis_reasoner import graph` returns a compiled object without further initialisation.
criteria_verification.evidence: Module exposes `graph: CompiledStateGraph[Any, Any, Any, Any]` as a module-level binding initialised by `_build_graph()` at import. _build_graph() calls `create_deep_agent(model=model, tools=[], system_prompt=ROLE_PROMPTS[role], subagents=[])` once per RoleName and wraps them in an outer StateGraph. Verified by TestAC001GraphCompiledAtImport (4 tests including isinstance(graph, CompiledStateGraph)) and confirmed by direct import smoke `from jarvis.agents.subagents.jarvis_reasoner import graph` returning a CompiledStateGraph object.
criteria_verification.notes: Module exposes `graph: CompiledStateGraph[Any, Any, Any, Any]` as a module-level binding initialised by `_build_graph()` at import. _build_graph() calls `create_deep_agent(model=model, tools=[], system_prompt=ROLE_PROMPTS[role], subagents=[])` once per RoleName and wraps them in an outer StateGraph. Verified by TestAC001GraphCompiledAtImport (4 tests including isinstance(graph, CompiledStateGraph)) and confirmed by direct import smoke `from jarvis.agents.subagents.jarvis_reasoner import graph` returning a CompiledStateGraph object.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: The first node of the graph reads `input["role"]` (or equivalent initial-state channel), looks up `ROLE_PROMPTS[RoleName(role)]`, and injects it as the system prompt.
criteria_verification.evidence: _resolve_role is set as entry point via builder.set_entry_point('resolve_role') and reads state.get('role'). Three role-specific dispatcher nodes are wired with system prompts pre-resolved from ROLE_PROMPTS[role] (one create_deep_agent per RoleName). Conditional edges route by resolved_role value to the matching dispatcher whose pre-resolved system_prompt is ROLE_PROMPTS[role]. Verified by TestAC002FirstNodeResolvesRole (parametrized over critic/researcher/planner) and TestAC009LeafGraphInvariants.test_create_deep_agent_called_with_each_role_prompt which asserts the set of system_prompts matches ROLE_PROMPTS values exactly.
criteria_verification.notes: _resolve_role is set as entry point via builder.set_entry_point('resolve_role') and reads state.get('role'). Conditional edges route by resolved_role value to the matching dispatcher whose pre-resolved system_prompt is ROLE_PROMPTS[role]. Verified by TestAC002FirstNodeResolvesRole (parametrized over critic/researcher/planner) and TestAC009LeafGraphInvariants.test_create_deep_agent_called_with_each_role_prompt which asserts the set of system_prompts matches ROLE_PROMPTS values exactly.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `OPENAI_BASE_URL` environment variable is set to `<config.llama_swap_base_url>/v1` at graph construction; the supervisor process sets this via lifecycle (no hard-coded URL in this module).
criteria_verification.evidence: The jarvis_reasoner module never assigns to os.environ and contains no http URL literal in executable code (docstring mentions only). Verified by TestAC003NoHardCodedBaseUrl which strips the module docstring and asserts no `http://`, no `promaxgb10`, no `9000/v1`, no `os.environ[]`, no `os.environ.setdefault`, no `os.putenv` in the executable source.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unknown role (e.g. `"bard"`, `"CRITIC"`, `"adversarial"`) returns structured error via `async_tasks` state channel: `"ERROR: unknown_role — expected one of {critic, researcher, planner}, got=<value>"`. **Never raises.**
criteria_verification.evidence: _resolve_role wraps `RoleName(role_value)` in try/except ValueError; on failure it returns _structured_error with the message `ERROR: unknown_role — expected one of {critic, researcher, planner}, got=<repr(value)>` populated on `async_tasks`, `messages`, and `error` state channels. The conditional-edge router short-circuits to END so no dispatcher runs. Verified by TestAC004UnknownRoleStructuredError parametrized over bard/CRITIC/Critic/adversarial/deep_reasoner plus an explicit test that invocation never raises.
criteria_verification.notes: _resolve_role wraps `RoleName(role_value)` in try/except ValueError; on failure it returns _structured_error with the message `ERROR: unknown_role — expected one of {critic, researcher, planner}, got=<repr(value)>` populated on `async_tasks`, `messages`, and `error` state channels. criteria_verification.criterion_text: Empty-string role (`""`) — `RoleName("")` raises `ValueError` at enum lookup; the graph catches and maps to the `unknown_role` branch (ASSUM-004, confirmed).
criteria_verification.evidence: The role==None check uses `is None` (not falsiness) so an empty string falls through to RoleName(role_value) which raises ValueError per default Python enum semantics; the except clause maps it onto the unknown_role error string with `got=''`. Verified by TestAC005EmptyStringRoleMapsToUnknownRole asserting the output starts with `ERROR: unknown_role` and contains `got=''`, plus a test that the invocation never raises.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Missing llama-swap alias (adapter-level failure) surfaces as structured error mentioning the `/running` endpoint.
criteria_verification.evidence: _make_role_runner wraps `await deep_agent.ainvoke(...)` in try/except Exception; on failure it emits an error string `ERROR: llama_swap_unavailable — model alias 'jarvis-reasoner' could not be resolved against the llama-swap /running endpoint (role=<role>, detail=<exc>)`. Verified by TestAC008LlamaSwapAliasMissing which builds a synthetic deep agent whose ainvoke raises, runs the dispatcher node, and asserts the output contains both `/running` and `llama_swap_unavailable` and that correlation_id propagates.

- Phase 3: `the stack-specific Phase-3 specialist` (Implementation)

context_used:
## Job-Specific Context

Budget: 1219/5200 tokens

### ✅ Similar Outcomes
- REFERENCED_IN
- USES_LIBRARY

### 🎨 Relevant Patterns
- CALLED_BY
- DISPLAYS_FILE_PATH

### ⚠️ Warnings
- IS_IMPLEMENTED_AS
- ALTERNATIVE_TO

### 🎭 Role Constraints
- MONITORS_RESULTS_FROM
- MONITOR_QUALITY_GATES_RESULTS_FROM_JSON

### 🛠️ Implementation Modes
- IS_INVOCATION_METHOD_FOR
